label: Neon
description: Connect to a Neon serverless Postgres database using a connection string or individual credentials.

form.title: Connect Neon
form.subtitle: Link Anton to your Neon serverless Postgres database.

methods.label: Connection String
methods.description: Paste the full Postgres connection string from the Neon Console. The fastest way to connect — no separate fields needed.
methods.how_to:
## Get your Neon connection string

1. Open the [Neon Console](https://console.neon.tech/) and select your **Project**.
2. Click **Connection Details** in the left sidebar (or the dashboard overview card).
3. In the **Connection string** panel, select the **branch**, **role**, and **database** you want Anton to use.
4. Click the **copy** icon to copy the full connection string. It looks like:
   `postgresql://username:password@ep-xxxx.us-east-2.aws.neon.tech/dbname?sslmode=require`
5. Paste it into the **Connection String** field below.

## Notes

- The string includes your password in plain text — keep it secret and do not commit it to source control.
- Neon generates a unique hostname per **branch**. If you create a new branch, you'll need a new connection string for it.
- `sslmode=require` is set by default in Neon's strings — do not remove it.
- If your password contains special characters, they will be URL-encoded in the string automatically by the Neon Console.

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Full Postgres connection URI from Neon Console → Project → Connection Details.

methods.label: Host / Port / Database / User / Password
methods.description: Enter each connection parameter separately. Useful when you already have the individual host, port, database name, and credentials from the Neon Console.
methods.how_to:
## Find your Neon connection parameters

1. Open the [Neon Console](https://console.neon.tech/) and select your **Project**.
2. Click **Connection Details** in the left sidebar.
3. Select the **branch**, **role**, and **database** you want to connect to.
4. Switch to the **Parameters only** view (or expand the connection string manually) to find:
   - **Host**: something like `ep-xxxx.us-east-2.aws.neon.tech`
   - **Port**: `5432` (Neon's default)
   - **Database**: the database name you selected
   - **User**: the role name you selected
   - **Password**: copy it from the masked field by clicking the eye or copy icon
5. Fill in each field below.

## Notes

- Always use SSL (`sslmode=require`) when connecting to Neon — leave **Use SSL** enabled.
- The password is shown once per session in the Console; if you lose it, reset the role's password under **Settings → Roles**.
- Port is almost always `5432` for Neon; only change it if your project uses a custom proxy.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: The Neon endpoint hostname from Connection Details.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database
fields.description: The name of the database to connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: User
fields.description: The Postgres role (user) name from Neon Console → Connection Details.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: The role's password. Find or reset it in Neon Console → Settings → Roles.

fields.name: ssl
fields.label: Use SSL (sslmode=require)
fields.description: Neon requires SSL. Keep this enabled unless you know what you're doing.
